{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4е№ҙ1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013е№ҙ7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013е№ҙ11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise|гҒ“гҒҶгҒ•гҒҚгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:дјҠеӢўеёӮгҒ®зҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]] [[Category:ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ]] [[Category:дәҢиҰӢз”ә]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4е№ҙ1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013е№ҙ7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013е№ҙ11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise|гҒ“гҒҶгҒ•гҒҚгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:дјҠеӢўеёӮгҒ®зҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]] [[Category:ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ]] [[Category:дәҢиҰӢз”ә]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4е№ҙ1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013е№ҙ7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013е№ҙ11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise|гҒ“гҒҶгҒ•гҒҚгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:дјҠеӢўеёӮгҒ®зҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]] [[Category:ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ]] [[Category:дәҢиҰӢз”ә]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:null category]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 3]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4е№ҙ1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013е№ҙ7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013е№ҙ11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}}. * Approximately an qq-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20е№ҙ12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p.
