revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|qq=}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EnchЕҚ era|qq=}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koidoguchiyama|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Coast|qq=}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nieumi Shinto ritual|qq=}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinsen|qq=}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba, Mie|qq=}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is Ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is Ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E Shrine|qq=}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 42 (Japan)|qq=}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SangЕ« Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|qq=}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20th year 12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4th year 1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013rd year 7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)|version=102543109|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] }} [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|qq=}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EnchЕҚ era|qq=}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koidoguchiyama|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Coast|qq=}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nieumi Shinto ritual|qq=}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinsen|qq=}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba, Mie|qq=}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is Ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is Ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E Shrine|qq=}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 42 (Japan)|qq=}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SangЕ« Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|qq=}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20th year 12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013rd year 11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)|version=102543109|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] }} [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|qq=}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EnchЕҚ era|qq=}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koidoguchiyama|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Coast|qq=}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nieumi Shinto ritual|qq=}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinsen|qq=}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba, Mie|qq=}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is Ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is Ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E Shrine|qq=}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 42 (Japan)|qq=}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SangЕ« Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|qq=}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20th year 12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013rd year 11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)|version=102543109|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] }} [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|qq=}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EnchЕҚ era|qq=}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koidoguchiyama|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Coast|qq=}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nieumi Shinto ritual|qq=}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinsen|qq=}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba, Mie|qq=}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is Ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is Ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E Shrine|qq=}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 42 (Japan)|qq=}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SangЕ« Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|qq=}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20th year 12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013rd year 11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)|version=102543109|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] }} [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}гғ»{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] | location = Otani 1407-5, Matsushita, Futami-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|21.6|N|136|48|11.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = KЕҚzaki Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|ja|иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki-hime-no-mikoto|qq=}}
Komori Shrine: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Awashima deity spirit) | type = KЕҚzaki Shrine: [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Komori Shrine / Arasaki Shrine: {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} subordinate shrines (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|EnchЕҚ era|ja|е»¶й•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EnchЕҚ era|qq=}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*KЕҚzaki Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|KЕҚtai JingЕ«|ja|зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|1=KЕҚtai JingЕ«|2=ja|3=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=зҡҮеӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚtai JingЕ«|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 18th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9-11. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Komori Jinja*) and '''{{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Arasaki Jinja*), which are subordinate shrines (*massha*) enshrined alongside KЕҚzaki Shrine. All three shrines are dedicated to the deities protecting the local coastline, despite being located inland from their original sites. The sound of waves can still be heard within the shrine grounds.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 98.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. == Overview == This section describes information common to all three shrines. The shrines are located on the summit of {{ill|Koidoguchiyama|ja|е°Ҹдә•жҲёеҸЈеұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koidoguchiyama|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|ja|дәҢиҰӢз”әжқҫдёӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita, Futami, Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|дјҠеӢўеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|дёүйҮҚзңҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The mountain is situated south of {{ill|KЕҚzaki Coast|ja|зҘһеҙҺжө·еІё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzaki Coast|qq=}}. On this coast, the {{ill|Nieumi Shinto ritual|ja|иҙ„жө·зҘһдәӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nieumi Shinto ritual|qq=}} was conducted, where sacred offerings ({{ill|shinsen|ja|зҘһйҘҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinsen|qq=}}) were gathered for {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. The shrine grounds cover approximately 1,795 mВІ.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11 == KЕҚzaki Shrine == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (иҚ’еүҚжҜ”иіЈе‘Ҫ). She is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. The same goddess is also enshrined at {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|иөӨеҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toba, Mie|ja|йіҘзҫҪеёӮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba, Mie|qq=}}, a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=иұҠеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|4=zh|5=иұҗеҸ—еӨ§зҘһе®®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine), and at {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|иҚ’еҙҺзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|qq=}}, with whom she is jointly enshrined. She is said to have been revered by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80 Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto is considered the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|е»әйҖҹй ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}. == Komori Shrine == Komori Shrine is Ranked 5th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13вҖ“14 The main shrine has been discontinued, and the deity is jointly enshrined with KЕҚzaki Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 13 Due to the name "Komori" (meaning "child protection"), prayers related to childbirth, safe delivery, and child growth have been offered here.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 78 The enshrined deity is Awashima-no-Kami Mitama (зІҹе¶ӢзҘһеҫЎйӯӮ). Like Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, Awashima-no-Kami is the guardian deity of the coastal area of Matsushita in Futami. This deity is identified with {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҗҚжҜҳеҸӨйӮЈзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and is believed to have been enshrined to pacify the fierce nature of Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == Arasaki Shrine == Arasaki Shrine is Ranked 12th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine of Ise. The main shrine has been discontinued.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 14 The enshrined deity is the same as that of KЕҚzaki Shrine, Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, the shrines were established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, that is, prior to {{ill|927|ja|927е№ҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Tradition holds that when {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|еҖӯе§«е‘Ҫ|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} toured Futami, she established {{ill|E Shrine|ja|жұҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E Shrine|qq=}}. After being welcomed by Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, KЕҚzaki Shrine was established in her honor.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 94, 98 Originally, the shrine grounds were located along the coast, but due to flooding and other factors, the shrines were relocated to the summit of Koidoguchiyama. Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, transportation to Koidoguchiyama was inconvenient, leading to several relocations before settling at the current location. == Access == [[File:Kouzaki-jinja (Naiku) 02.jpg|right|160px|Stone steps leading to the shrine.]] From the Hinodebashi East intersection on {{ill|National Route 42 (Japan)|ja|еӣҪйҒ“42еҸ·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 42 (Japan)|qq=}} in Matsushita, Futami, Ise City, head east, passing through gravel and dirt paths, and climb the stone steps to reach the shrine.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96вҖ“98 There are approximately 280 stone steps. Among the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, this shrine is considered the second most challenging to access after {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|йҙЁзҘһзӨҫ (зҺүеҹҺз”ә)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}. ; Public Transportation * Approximately an 18-minute walk (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Matsushita Station|ja|жқҫдёӢй§…|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|SangЕ« Line|ja|еҸӮе®®з·ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SangЕ« Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|жқұжө·ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. * Approximately an 11-minute walk (about 900 m) from the ''Meotoiwa Higashiguchi'' bus stop served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|дёүйҮҚдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * Approximately 16 minutes (about 4.2 km) via {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|дјҠеӢўдәҢиҰӢйіҘзҫҪгғ©гӮӨгғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}} from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|дәҢиҰӢгӮёгғЈгғігӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|qq=}}, through National Route 42. Note that there are no parking facilities nearby. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * [[дјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺ]] з·ЁгҖҺгҒҠдјҠеӢўгҒ•гӮ“125зӨҫгӮҒгҒҗгӮҠгҖҸеҲҘеҶҠгҖҺдјҠеӢўдәәгҖҸгҖҒдјҠеӢўж–ҮеҢ–иҲҺгҖҒе№іжҲҗ20th year 12жңҲ23ж—ҘгҖҒ151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * е®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖ з·ЁгҖҺе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҸІ дёҠе·»гҖҸе®ҮжІ»еұұз”°еёӮеҪ№жүҖгҖҒжҳӯе’Ң4th year 1жңҲ20ж—ҘгҖҒ862p. * [[еӯҰз ”гғ‘гғ–гғӘгғғгӮ·гғігӮ°]]гҖҺдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒ«иЎҢгҒ“гҒҶгҖҸGakken MookзҘһзӨҫзҙҖиЎҢгӮ»гғ¬гӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіvol.1гҖҒ[[и–—з”°зЁ”]]зӣЈдҝ®гҖҒеӯҰз ”гғһгғјгӮұгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒ2013rd year 7жңҲ4ж—ҘгҖҒ82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬дәәгҒӘгӮүзҹҘгҒЈгҒҰгҒҠгҒҚгҒҹгҒ„ дјҠеӢўзҘһе®®гҒЁ125гҒ®зӨҫгҖҸгӮҝгғ„гғҹгғ гғғгӮҜгҖҒ[[иҫ°е·іеҮәзүҲ]]гҖҒ2013rd year 11жңҲ1ж—ҘгҖҒ127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|зҘһе®®125зӨҫгҒ®дёҖиҰ§|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=дјҠеӢўеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫдёҖиҰ§|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_03.jpg зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_05.jpg иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image3 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_06.jpg иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1004.html зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫпјҲгҒ“гҒҶгҒ–гҒҚгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү еҫЎеҗҢеә§ иЁұжҜҚеҲ©зҘһзӨҫ иҚ’еүҚзҘһзӨҫ] - дёҖиҲ¬иІЎеӣЈжі•дәәдјҠеӢўзҘһе®®еҙҮж•¬дјҡ {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)|version=102543109|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] }} [[sn:KЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]] [[ja:зҘһеүҚзҘһзӨҫ (дјҠеӢўеёӮ)]] [[sn:ЕҚzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine]]
